source_jobs.title: Future Openings
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATROPOS HEALTH

Atropos Health is the developer of GENEVA™ OS , the operating system for rapid healthcare evidence across a robust network of real-world data. Healthcare and life science organizations work with Atropos Health to close evidence gaps from bench to bedside, improving individual patient outcomes with data-driven care, expediting research that advances the field of medicine, and more. We aim to transform healthcare with timely, relevant real-world evidence.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Please note: this is not an official job.

We're always looking for world-class talent to join our team!

If you do not see an open role applicable to your skillset, and you have an interest in joining Atropos Health, please apply and we will store your information for any relevant roles that open up in the future.




LOCATION

We are a remote first company! All applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis and must not require, now or in the future, employer sponsorship. At this time, we are unable to hire in the following states and territories: Wyoming, North Dakota, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, and the U.S. Virgin Islands.




OUR CORE VALUES

 - Bias to Action. We are driven by urgency and persistence, ensuring our actions contribute to the relentless pursuit of our mission.

 - Intellectual Honesty. We are committed to the integrity of our work, seeking truth, and building transparent methodologies that go beyond industry norms.

 - Compelled by Curiosity. We seek to solve the unknown, push boundaries, and do not accept the status quo.

 - Impactful Innovation. We pioneer solutions that pursue equity and representation that have a transformative impact for our customers, users, and patients.

 - Mindful Mentorship. We approach all interactions with an empathetic understanding, actively investing in the growth of each other and our customers.




PERKS

 -  Health Benefits. Our benefit package includes employer paid Medical, Dental, Vision,  Life, STD, and LTD insurance. We also offer FSA.

 - Parental Leave. We offer up to twelve weeks of paid leave for new parents who have been at the company for 6+ months.

 - Financial Wellness. Save for retirement through our 401k plan with Human Interest.

 - Flexible Work Environment. We're a remote first company with a flexible vacation policy.

 - Home Office Stipend. All full-time employees will receive a stipend to support their remote office setup.

 - Offsites. As a remote company we take time 2-3 times a year to get together in small teams and all together as a company. Attendance at scheduled company offsites is a component of this role and is expected of all team members.





At Atropos, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace where every individual feels valued, respected, and empowered to contribute their unique perspectives and talents. We are an equal-opportunity employer that does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, age, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, genetics, disability, pregnancy, veteran status, or any other legally protected status.



Atropos participates in E-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S. only after a job offer is accepted and Form I-9 is completed. For more information on E-Verify, please visit the following: EVerify Participation https://nam12.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.e-verify.gov%2Fsites%2Fdefault%2Ffiles%2Feverify%2Fposters%2FEVerifyParticipationPoster.pdf&data=05%7C01%7Cjill.steger%40talkiatry.com%7Cfe829943872e4bc8dc7b08db83d3c99d%7C803a6c90ec724384b2c01a376841a04b%7C0%7C0%7C638248717875458398%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=qFgJNzh246Js%2FFqdsb9OZgN55rg5GpsB0wjiTvkPn9w%3D&reserved=0 & IER Right to Work https://nam12

source_jobs.title: Staff Sales Engineer (Life Sciences)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATROPOS HEALTH

Atropos Health is the developer of GENEVA™ OS , the operating system for rapid healthcare evidence across a robust network of real-world data. Healthcare and life science organizations work with Atropos Health to close evidence gaps from bench to bedside, improving individual patient outcomes with data-driven care, expediting research that advances the field of medicine, and more. We aim to transform healthcare with timely, relevant real-world evidence.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Atropos Health is seeking a dynamic Staff Sales Engineer to join our growing Commercial team. The Staff Sales Engineer role is an externally-facing, revenue-focused role, responsible for working with the Sales team to demonstrate the value of Atropos Health’s solutions to Life Sciences prospects and clients. The Staff Sales Engineer researches customers, understands their needs, and aligns Atropos Health’s solutions to those needs. They are responsible for the creation of presentation content, including demos, and will be integral to present the solution to customers during meetings.

The ideal candidate for this role has prior experience selling and delivering real world evidence and precision medicine solutions to life sciences and pharmaceutical companies and has a deep understanding of the healthcare industry, data, technology, and analytical methods. This candidate must have extremely strong communication skills and must be able to clearly and concisely articulate complex ideas and concepts common to the healthcare industry. This person is expected to be a subject matter expert and must conduct themselves with poise, often pivoting in real time to meet customer needs.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate with the sales team to identify and engage potential Life Science clients, understanding their unique needs and pain points.

 - Deliver compelling product demonstrations and presentations that highlight the value proposition of Atropos Health’s products and services.

 - Serve as a technical resource during the selling process, addressing client queries and providing in-depth knowledge of solution capabilities. This includes learning Atropos’ Temporal Query Language and applying it to the curation of cohorts and the generation or preliminary analyses.

 - Understand pricing, customer value, and pricing strategy to articulate ROI to customers during the sales process.

 - Work closely with product management and engineering teams to convey customer feedback and influence product development, ensuring alignment with market demands.

 - Develop and maintain strong relationships with key customer stakeholders.

 - Participate in industry conferences, workshops, and webinars to enhance awareness of Atropos Health’s offerings and expand professional networks.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills are required for this role.

 - Strong initiative and the ability to work independently.

 - Prior experience working with real-world healthcare data (EMR, claims, etc.) and deep knowledge of its advantages, shortcomings, and use cases is required.

 - Demonstrated experience working with a sales team.

 - Strong technical skills and hands-on experience with SQL is required. Additional coding languages (e.g., R, Python) is a plus.

 - Prior experience working and proven aptitude in a startup environment.

 - An advanced degree in a clinical setting (e.g., clinical informatics, Ph.D http://Ph.DM, PharmD, MPH, M.D.) is preferred.

 - 7+years work experience in healthcare, consulting, or pharmaceuticals/biotech in a customer facing role is required.




REPORTING

The Sales Engineer reports directly to the Vice President of Sales Engineering, Vinny Marino https://www.linkedin.com/in/vinny-marino-25790132/.




LOCATION

We are a remote first company! All applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis and must not require, now or in the future, employer sponsorshi

source_jobs.title: Medical Innovation Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATROPOS HEALTH

Atropos Health is the developer of GENEVA™ OS , the operating system for rapid healthcare evidence across a robust network of real-world data. Healthcare and life science organizations work with Atropos Health to close evidence gaps from bench to bedside, improving individual patient outcomes with data-driven care, expediting research that advances the field of medicine, and more. We aim to transform healthcare with timely, relevant real-world evidence.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Atropos Health is looking for a curious, technically skilled individual who thrives at the intersection of clinical knowledge and generative AI. This role is less about checking boxes on a résumé and more about what you can build. If your GitHub, Kaggle, Hugging Face, or Substack tells a compelling story, we want to hear from you.

You will work directly with our Medical Director of Content Generation to develop and scale a pipeline capable of answering clinical and research questions across hundreds of conditions and interventions. This includes producing structured, metataged content optimized for search and retrieval, sourcing and synthesizing external evidence (published studies, clinical trials, payor policies, CMS Local Coverage Determinations, and common patient/clinician questions), and integrating this content with Atropos's real-world evidence (RWE) platform.

You will be a core contributor to our ability to generate high-quality, scalable clinical intelligence — using LLMs and generative AI tools as force multipliers throughout the process.

This role does not involve providing medical advice or clinical services.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Partner with the Medical Director of Content Generation to build and maintain a scalable pipeline for answering clinical and research questions across diverse disease areas and interventions

 - Develop and curate a library of common clinician and consumer questions spanning a wide range of clinical conditions — from common to rare

 - Convert clinical questions into structured phenotypes or ontological representations (e.g., ICD-10 codes, RxNORM, LOINC, CPT)

 - Metatag produced content to enable optimized search and retrieval across the Atropos platform

 - Source and integrate external content: published studies, active clinical trials, payor policies, CMS Local Coverage Determinations, and consumer-facing clinical questions

 - Verify study outputs against published literature and participate in output QA/QC processes

 - Produce reusable, well-documented code and content that can be integrated into internal tools and surfaced to external stakeholders through product and engineering collaboration

 - Use LLMs and generative AI tools to produce a variety of outputs: code, interactive widgets, PowerPoint presentations, PDFs, and markdown documents

 - Build and consume internal and external APIs; contribute to the development of APIs for Atropos products

 - Work cross-functionally with clinical, engineering, product, and customer success teams




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - Daily hands-on practice with Python, R, or SQL — either natively or preferably in conjunction with generative AI tools (e.g., Claude, GPT-4, Cursor, Copilot)

 - Demonstrated ability to iterate toward high-quality outputs through structured, self-directed quality checks — owning the end-to-end process

 - Working knowledge of clinical terminology standards (ICD-10, CPT, SNOMED, LOINC, RxNORM/NDC) and ability to rapidly learn the clinical nuances of how these ontologies are applied in real-world healthcare

 - Ability to produce clean, reusable code and content that others can build on

 - Strong written and oral communication skills in English

 - Ability to work within standard US business hours (any US time zone)




REPORTING

The Medical Innovation Specialist reports to Medical Director, Content.




LOCATION

We are a remote first company! All applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States on

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (Health System)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATROPOS HEALTH

Atropos Health is the developer of GENEVA™ OS , the operating system for rapid healthcare evidence across a robust network of real-world data. Healthcare and life science organizations work with Atropos Health to close evidence gaps from bench to bedside, improving individual patient outcomes with data-driven care, expediting research that advances the field of medicine, and more. We aim to transform healthcare with timely, relevant real-world evidence.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Atropos Health is seeking a motivated and results-driven Sales Development Representative (SDR) to join our commercial sales team. In this role, you will be responsible for generating and qualifying leads to build a strong Health System sales pipeline. You’ll work closely with the sales and marketing teams to identify target prospects, initiate contact through various outbound channels, and set up discovery meetings. The ideal candidate is a quick learner, highly organized, and passionate about transforming healthcare through real-world evidence. This position requires excellent communications skills, a proactive attitude, and the ability to thrive in a fast paced environment.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Identify and research prospective customers within healthcare system companies.

 - Conduct outbound outreach via email, phone, LinkedIn, and other channels to generate new sales opportunities.

 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads to determine the potential fit for Atropos Health’s solutions.

 - Schedule and coordinate discovery calls between prospects and Sales SVP/-VP-Directors .

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date records in the CRM system (Hubspot)

 - Collaborate with Marketing to optimize outreach strategies and sequences.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelors Degree in Business, Marketing or a related field

 - 2+ years of experience in a prior SDR role calling on Health System accounts

 - Proven track record of exceeding performance metrics (meetings booked, pipeline generated)

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to craft clear, compelling outreach messages

 - Regularly demonstrates professional persistence when daily prospecting

 - Comfortable with cold calling, email outreach, and using LinkedIn for prospecting to executive-level stakeholders

 - Familiarity with CRM tools (Hubspot) and sales prospecting tools (ZoomInfo, Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator)

 - Passionate about healthcare and technology

 - Ability to work in a virtual environment

 - Ability to work collaboratively across teams and take constructive feedback

 - Has a professional demeanor and a growth-oriented mindset




REPORTING

The Sales Development Representative reports directly to the Manager, Sales Operations Michael Dnistran http://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-dnistran-1a2a54198




LOCATION

We are a remote first company! All applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis and must not require, now or in the future, employer sponsorship.
